mechanism_narrative: A low and stable unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates a healthy and robust labor market, which supports strong consumer spending and corporate profitability, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 40% of periods where UNRATE is below its 12-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: A significant acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand and economic expansion, which translates directly into higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances where RSAFS 3-month growth exceeds 1%.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is maintained at a low level and industrial production (INDPRO) shows consistent growth, it signals an accommodative monetary environment supporting a nascent economic rebound, which boosts equity valuations and leads to positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.10) when both FEDFUNDS is low and INDPRO growth is positive.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening labor market, indicated by a persistently low unemployment rate (UNRATE), wage pressures can intensify, eroding corporate profit margins and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns as earnings expectations decline.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of late-cycle periods where UNRATE is below 5%.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals weakening manufacturing activity and broader economic contraction, which typically leads to lower corporate profits and negative investor sentiment, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 35% of triggered instances where INDPRO 3-month growth falls below -0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent low inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, allows the central bank to maintain accommodative monetary policies, which supports higher equity valuations by lowering discount rates and encouraging investment, leading to positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with low CPIAUCSL over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) combined with robust payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) indicate broad-based economic strength and rising consumer confidence, which translates into higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns are negative or flat (within +/- 0.1%) in more than 45% of periods where both housing and employment show strong growth.

mechanism_narrative: A significant deceleration in GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates a weakening economy and potential recessionary pressures, which typically leads to lower corporate profits, reduced investor confidence, and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 30% of periods where GDPC1 year-over-year growth falls below 1.5%.
